    18вҶ’ */
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <chrono>
    23вҶ’#include <cstddef>
    24вҶ’#include <deque>
    25вҶ’#include <memory>
    26вҶ’#include <mutex>
    27вҶ’#include <string>
    28вҶ’#include <unordered_map>
    29вҶ’#include <vector>
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/// A single message held in the buffer, waiting to be aggregated and sent.
    34вҶ’struct BufferedMessage {
    35вҶ’  uint64_t timestamp_ns;    ///< Source-clock timestamp from the middleware
    36вҶ’  uint64_t received_at_ns;  ///< Wall-clock time when add_message() was called (used for TTL cleanup)
    37вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> data;  ///< CDR-encoded message payload
    38вҶ’};
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’/// Thread-safe per-topic message buffer with time-based auto-cleanup.
    41вҶ’///
    42вҶ’/// Messages arrive via add_message() (called from the SubscriptionManager callback)
    43вҶ’/// and are drained by move_messages() (called from BridgeServer::publish_aggregated_messages()).
    44вҶ’///
    45вҶ’/// Stale messages older than max_message_age_ns are automatically removed on each
    46вҶ’/// add_message() call. Cleanup uses received_at_ns (wall-clock), not timestamp_ns,
    47вҶ’/// so sim-time offsets don't cause premature eviction or unbounded growth.
    48вҶ’///
    49вҶ’/// Thread safety: all public methods are mutex-protected. add_message() may be
    50вҶ’/// called concurrently with move_messages() from different threads.
    51вҶ’class MessageBuffer {
    52вҶ’ public:
    53вҶ’  static constexpr uint64_t kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs = 1'000'000'000;  ///< 1 second
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’  /// @param max_message_age_ns  TTL for buffered messages (default 1 second).
    56вҶ’  explicit MessageBuffer(uint64_t max_message_age_ns = kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs);
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’  /// Add a message to the buffer for the given topic.
    59вҶ’  /// Triggers cleanup of stale messages before inserting.
    60вҶ’  /// @param topic_name    fully-qualified topic name
    61вҶ’  /// @param timestamp_ns  source-clock timestamp from the middleware
    62вҶ’  /// @param data          CDR-encoded message payload (shared ownership)
    63вҶ’  void add_message(const std::string& topic_name, uint64_t timestamp_ns, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> data);
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’  /// Atomically drain all buffered messages into @p out_messages.
    66вҶ’  /// After this call the internal buffer is empty. Messages are appended to any
    67вҶ’  /// existing entries in out_messages (keyed by topic name).
    68вҶ’  void move_messages(std::unordered_map<std::string, std::deque<BufferedMessage>>& out_messages);
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’  /// Discard all buffered messages across all topics.
    71вҶ’  void clear();
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’  /// Return the total number of messages across all topics.
    74вҶ’  size_t size() const;
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’ private:
    77вҶ’  mutable std::mutex mutex_;
    78вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, std::deque<BufferedMessage>> topic_buffers_;
    79вҶ’  uint64_t last_read_timestamp_ns_;
    80вҶ’  uint64_t max_message_age_ns_;
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’  void cleanup_old_messages();
    83вҶ’};
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <cstddef>
    23вҶ’#include <cstdint>
    24вҶ’#include <string>
    25вҶ’#include <vector>
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’/**
    30вҶ’ * @brief Serializes aggregated messages using streaming zero-copy approach
    31вҶ’ *
    32вҶ’ * Design principles:
    33вҶ’ * - Streaming API: Messages serialized immediately to output buffer (no intermediate storage)
    34вҶ’ * - No header: Messages serialized sequentially without placeholder or count header
    35вҶ’ * - Little-endian: All multi-byte values use little-endian byte order
    36вҶ’ *
    37вҶ’ * Binary format specification (per message):
    38вҶ’ *   - Topic name length (uint16_t little-endian)
    39вҶ’ *   - Topic name (N bytes UTF-8)
    40вҶ’ *   - Timestamp (uint64_t nanoseconds since epoch, little-endian)
    41вҶ’ *   - Message data length (uint32_t little-endian)
    42вҶ’ *   - Message data (N bytes - CDR serialized)
    43вҶ’ *
    44вҶ’ * Thread safety: Not thread-safe, use from single thread only
    45вҶ’ */
    46вҶ’class AggregatedMessageSerializer {
    47вҶ’ public:
    48вҶ’  AggregatedMessageSerializer();
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  /**
    51вҶ’   * @brief Serialize a message directly to the output buffer
    52вҶ’   *
    53вҶ’   * @param topic_name Topic name
    54вҶ’   * @param timestamp_ns Timestamp in nanoseconds since epoch
    55вҶ’   * @param data Pointer to CDR serialized message bytes
    56вҶ’   * @param data_size Size of the message data in bytes
    57вҶ’   */
    58вҶ’  void serialize_message(const std::string& topic_name, uint64_t timestamp_ns, const std::byte* data, size_t data_size);
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’  /**
    61вҶ’   * @brief Get reference to the serialized data buffer
    62вҶ’   */
    63вҶ’  const std::vector<uint8_t>& get_serialized_data() const {
    64вҶ’    return serialized_data_;
    65вҶ’  }
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’  /**
    68вҶ’   * @brief Clear the buffer for next aggregation cycle
    69вҶ’   */
    70вҶ’  void clear();
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’  /**
    73вҶ’   * @brief Get number of messages added so far
    74вҶ’   */
    75вҶ’  size_t get_message_count() const;
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’  /**
    78вҶ’   * @brief Finalize the aggregated messages with header and compression
    79вҶ’   *
    80вҶ’   * Produces output with 16-byte header (uncompressed) followed by ZSTD-compressed payload:
    81вҶ’   *   - Offset 0: magic (uint32_t "PJRB" = 0x42524A50, little-endian)
    82вҶ’   *   - Offset 4: message_count (uint32_t, little-endian)
    83вҶ’   *   - Offset 8: uncompressed_size (uint32_t, little-endian)
    84вҶ’   *   - Offset 12: flags (uint32_t, reserved = 0)
    85вҶ’   *   - Offset 16+: ZSTD-compressed payload
    86вҶ’   *
    87вҶ’   * @return Vector containing header + compressed payload
    88вҶ’   */
    89вҶ’  std::vector<uint8_t> finalize();
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’  /**
    92вҶ’   * @brief Compress data using ZSTD (compression level 1)
    93вҶ’   */
    94вҶ’  static void compress_zstd(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::vector<uint8_t>& compressed_data);
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’  /**
    97вҶ’   * @brief Decompress ZSTD data
    98вҶ’   */
    99вҶ’  static void decompress_zstd(const std::vector<uint8_t>& compressed_data, std::vector<uint8_t>& decompressed);
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’ private:
   102вҶ’  std::vector<uint8_t> serialized_data_;
   103вҶ’  size_t message_count_{0};
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’  template <typename T>
   106вҶ’  static void write_le(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, T value);
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’  static void write_str(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, const std::string& str);
   109вҶ’};

   111вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    29вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/// Callback invoked when a new message arrives on a subscribed topic.
    32вҶ’/// @param topic_name  the topic the message was received on
    33вҶ’/// @param cdr_data    serialized message payload (CDR-encoded)
    34вҶ’/// @param timestamp_ns  nanoseconds since epoch (source-clock timestamp)
    35вҶ’using MessageCallback = std::function<void(
    36вҶ’    const std::string& topic_name, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, uint64_t timestamp_ns)>;
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’/// Abstract interface for managing middleware subscriptions with reference counting.
    39вҶ’///
    40вҶ’/// Implementations wrap a backend-specific subscription mechanism (e.g. rclcpp
    41вҶ’/// GenericSubscription for ROS2, DDS DataReader for RTI) behind a uniform API.
    42вҶ’///
    43вҶ’/// Subscriptions are reference-counted: multiple clients can subscribe to the
    44вҶ’/// same topic, and the underlying middleware subscription is only destroyed when
    45вҶ’/// the last client unsubscribes. Each subscribe() call increments the ref count;
    46вҶ’/// each unsubscribe() call decrements it.
    47вҶ’///
    48вҶ’/// A single global MessageCallback is set once at startup. All incoming messages
    49вҶ’/// from all topics are delivered through this callback, which feeds them into
    50вҶ’/// the MessageBuffer for later aggregation and delivery.
    51вҶ’///
    52вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from any thread.
    53вҶ’class SubscriptionManagerInterface {
    54вҶ’ public:
    55вҶ’  virtual ~SubscriptionManagerInterface() = default;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Set the global callback for all incoming messages. Must be called once
    58вҶ’  /// before any subscribe() call.
    59вҶ’  virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’  /// Subscribe to a topic, incrementing its reference count.
    62вҶ’  /// If this is the first subscriber, the underlying middleware subscription is created.
    63вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name (e.g. "/sensor/imu")
    64вҶ’  /// @param topic_type  message type string (e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu");
    65вҶ’  ///                    required by ROS2, ignored by DDS backends.
    66вҶ’  /// @return true on success, false if the subscription could not be created.
    67вҶ’  virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from a topic, decrementing its reference count.
    70вҶ’  /// The underlying middleware subscription is destroyed when the count reaches zero.
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if the topic was found and ref count decremented, false otherwise.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from all topics, destroying all underlying subscriptions.
    75вҶ’  /// Called during shutdown.
    76вҶ’  virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
    77вҶ’};
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
    80вҶ’

You are reviewing the pj_bridge project for correctness and potential bugs. Focus on:

- `app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp` and `app/include/pj_bridge/middleware/websocket_middleware.hpp`

Read both files completely. Then analyze for:

1. **Thread safety**: IXWebSocket callbacks run on separate threads. Check that all shared state (client map, queue, callbacks) is properly protected. Look for races between on_message callbacks and send/shutdown operations.

2. **Shutdown safety**: Can callbacks fire after shutdown() returns? Can send_reply/send_binary be called after shutdown? Is the server pointer safe during destruction?

3. **Resource leaks**: WebSocket connections not properly closed, queue growing unbounded, client map entries not cleaned up.

5. **Edge cases**: Rapid connect/disconnect, sending to a client that just disconnected, concurrent sends to the same client, empty data.

Do NOT report style issues. Only report actual bugs or correctness concerns. Cite exact file and line numbers. Explain the potential impact.

This is a research-only task вҖ” do NOT modify any files.

    33вҶ’/// Implementations handle connection management and bidirectional messaging.
    34вҶ’/// The interface separates two data channels:
    35вҶ’///   - **Text** (request/reply): JSON API commands (subscribe, heartbeat, etc.)
    36вҶ’///   - **Binary** (per-client push): ZSTD-compressed aggregated message frames
    37вҶ’///
    38вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from multiple threads.
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls receive_request() and send_reply() from the request-processing
    40вҶ’/// loop, and send_binary() from the publish loop, potentially concurrently.
    41вҶ’class MiddlewareInterface {
    42вҶ’ public:
    43вҶ’  virtual ~MiddlewareInterface() = default;
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’  /// Start listening on the given port. Must be called before any other method.
    46вҶ’  /// @return void on success, or an error string describing the failure.
    47вҶ’  virtual tl::expected<void, std::string> initialize(uint16_t port) = 0;
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’  /// Stop the server and close all client connections.
    50вҶ’  /// After this call, is_ready() must return false.
    51вҶ’  virtual void shutdown() = 0;
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’  /// Poll for the next incoming text request (non-blocking or short-blocking).
    54вҶ’  /// @param[out] data       the raw request bytes (UTF-8 JSON)
    55вҶ’  /// @param[out] client_identity  opaque client identifier for send_reply()
    56вҶ’  /// @return true if a request was dequeued, false if the queue was empty.
    57вҶ’  virtual bool receive_request(std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::string& client_identity) = 0;
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’  /// Send a text reply to a specific client.
    60вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    61вҶ’  virtual bool send_reply(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’  /// Broadcast binary data to all connected clients.
    64вҶ’  /// @return true if at least one client received the data.
    65вҶ’  virtual bool publish_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’  /// Send binary data to a specific client (used for per-client aggregated frames).
    68вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    69вҶ’  virtual bool send_binary(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if initialize() succeeded and shutdown() has not been called.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool is_ready() const = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  using ConnectionCallback = std::function<void(const std::string& client_id)>;
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a new client connects.
    77вҶ’  virtual void set_on_connect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a client disconnects.
    80вҶ’  /// BridgeServer uses this to trigger session cleanup.
    81вҶ’  virtual void set_on_disconnect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    82вҶ’};
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
    85вҶ’

    18вҶ’ */
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <string>
    23вҶ’#include <vector>
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’/// A discovered topic with its fully-qualified name and message type.
    28вҶ’struct TopicInfo {
    29вҶ’  std::string name;  ///< e.g. "/sensor/imu"
    30вҶ’  std::string type;  ///< e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu"
    31вҶ’};
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/// Abstract interface for discovering topics and retrieving their schemas.
    34вҶ’///
    35вҶ’/// Implementations wrap backend-specific discovery mechanisms:
    36вҶ’///   - ROS2: `Ros2TopicSource` uses rclcpp topic enumeration + .msg file parsing
    37вҶ’///   - RTI:  `RtiTopicSource` uses DDS participant discovery + OMG IDL type info
    38вҶ’///
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls get_topics() to list available topics for clients, and
    40вҶ’/// get_schema() before subscribing to provide the client with the schema needed
    41вҶ’/// to deserialize incoming messages.
    42вҶ’class TopicSourceInterface {
    43вҶ’ public:
    44вҶ’  virtual ~TopicSourceInterface() = default;
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’  /// Return all currently available topics.
    47вҶ’  /// May be called repeatedly (e.g. on each client "get_topics" request).
    48вҶ’  virtual std::vector<TopicInfo> get_topics() = 0;
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  /// Return the full schema definition for a topic.
    51вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name
    52вҶ’  /// @return the schema text (e.g. concatenated .msg definitions for ROS2,
    53вҶ’  ///         OMG IDL for RTI). Empty string if the schema cannot be resolved.
    54вҶ’  /// @throws std::exception on unrecoverable schema extraction errors.
    55вҶ’  virtual std::string get_schema(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Return the encoding identifier for schemas produced by this source.
    58вҶ’  /// Used in the subscribe response so clients know how to parse the schema.
    59вҶ’  /// @return "ros2msg" for ROS2, "omgidl" for RTI DDS.
    60вҶ’  virtual std::string schema_encoding() const = 0;
    61вҶ’};
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
    64вҶ’

    18вҶ’ */
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <cstddef>
    23вҶ’#include <cstdint>
    24вҶ’#include <string>
    25вҶ’#include <vector>
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’/**
    30вҶ’ * @brief Serializes aggregated messages using streaming zero-copy approach
    31вҶ’ *
    32вҶ’ * Design principles:
    33вҶ’ * - Streaming API: Messages serialized immediately to output buffer (no intermediate storage)
    34вҶ’ * - No header: Messages serialized sequentially without placeholder or count header
    35вҶ’ * - Little-endian: All multi-byte values use little-endian byte order
    36вҶ’ *
    37вҶ’ * Binary format specification (per message):
    38вҶ’ *   - Topic name length (uint16_t little-endian)
    39вҶ’ *   - Topic name (N bytes UTF-8)
    40вҶ’ *   - Timestamp (uint64_t nanoseconds since epoch, little-endian)
    41вҶ’ *   - Message data length (uint32_t little-endian)
    42вҶ’ *   - Message data (N bytes - CDR serialized)
    43вҶ’ *
    44вҶ’ * Thread safety: Not thread-safe, use from single thread only
    45вҶ’ */
    46вҶ’class AggregatedMessageSerializer {
    47вҶ’ public:
    48вҶ’  AggregatedMessageSerializer();
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  /**
    51вҶ’   * @brief Serialize a message directly to the output buffer
    52вҶ’   *
    53вҶ’   * @param topic_name Topic name
    54вҶ’   * @param timestamp_ns Timestamp in nanoseconds since epoch
    55вҶ’   * @param data Pointer to CDR serialized message bytes
    56вҶ’   * @param data_size Size of the message data in bytes
    57вҶ’   */
    58вҶ’  void serialize_message(const std::string& topic_name, uint64_t timestamp_ns, const std::byte* data, size_t data_size);
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’  /**
    61вҶ’   * @brief Get reference to the serialized data buffer
    62вҶ’   */
    63вҶ’  const std::vector<uint8_t>& get_serialized_data() const {
    64вҶ’    return serialized_data_;
    65вҶ’  }
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’  /**
    68вҶ’   * @brief Clear the buffer for next aggregation cycle
    69вҶ’   */
    70вҶ’  void clear();
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’  /**
    73вҶ’   * @brief Get number of messages added so far
    74вҶ’   */
    75вҶ’  size_t get_message_count() const;
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’  /**
    78вҶ’   * @brief Finalize the aggregated messages with header and compression
    79вҶ’   *
    80вҶ’   * Produces output with 16-byte header (uncompressed) followed by ZSTD-compressed payload:
    81вҶ’   *   - Offset 0: magic (uint32_t "PJRB" = 0x42524A50, little-endian)
    82вҶ’   *   - Offset 4: message_count (uint32_t, little-endian)
    83вҶ’   *   - Offset 8: uncompressed_size (uint32_t, little-endian)
    84вҶ’   *   - Offset 12: flags (uint32_t, reserved = 0)
    85вҶ’   *   - Offset 16+: ZSTD-compressed payload
    86вҶ’   *
    87вҶ’   * @return Vector containing header + compressed payload
    88вҶ’   */
    89вҶ’  std::vector<uint8_t> finalize();
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’  /**
    92вҶ’   * @brief Compress data using ZSTD (compression level 1)
    93вҶ’   */
    94вҶ’  static void compress_zstd(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::vector<uint8_t>& compressed_data);
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’  /**
    97вҶ’   * @brief Decompress ZSTD data
    98вҶ’   */
    99вҶ’  static void decompress_zstd(const std::vector<uint8_t>& compressed_data, std::vector<uint8_t>& decompressed);
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’ private:
   102вҶ’  std::vector<uint8_t> serialized_data_;
   103вҶ’  size_t message_count_{0};
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’  template <typename T>
   106вҶ’  static void write_le(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, T value);
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’  static void write_str(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, const std::string& str);
   109вҶ’};
   110вҶ’
   111вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
   112вҶ’

    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <cstddef>
    23вҶ’#include <cstdint>
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’/// Protocol version number, included in all JSON responses
    28вҶ’static constexpr int kProtocolVersion = 1;
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’/// Magic bytes for binary frame header: "PJRB" in little-endian
    31вҶ’static constexpr uint32_t kBinaryFrameMagic = 0x42524A50;
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/// Size of the binary frame header in bytes
    34вҶ’static constexpr size_t kBinaryHeaderSize = 16;
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’/// Schema encoding identifier for ROS2 message definitions
    37вҶ’static constexpr const char* kSchemaEncodingRos2Msg = "ros2msg";
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’/// Schema encoding identifier for OMG IDL type definitions
    40вҶ’static constexpr const char* kSchemaEncodingOmgIdl = "omgidl";
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <chrono>
    23вҶ’#include <cstdint>
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’inline uint64_t get_current_time_ns() {
    28вҶ’  auto now = std::chrono::system_clock::now();
    29вҶ’  auto duration = now.time_since_epoch();
    30вҶ’  return static_cast<uint64_t>(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::nanoseconds>(duration).count());
    31вҶ’}
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

1вҶ’/*
     2вҶ’ * Copyright (C) 2026 Davide Faconti
     3вҶ’ *
     4вҶ’ * This file is part of pj_bridge.
     5вҶ’ *
     6вҶ’ * pj_bridge is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify
     7вҶ’ * it under the terms of the GNU Affero General Public License as published by
     8вҶ’ * the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or
     9вҶ’ * (at your option) any later version.
    10вҶ’ *
    11вҶ’ * pj_bridge is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
    12вҶ’ * but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
    13вҶ’ * MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the
    14вҶ’ * GNU Affero General Public License for more details.
    15вҶ’ *
    16вҶ’ * You should have received a copy of the GNU Affero General Public License
    17вҶ’ * along with pj_bridge. If not, see <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
    18вҶ’ */
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <memory>
    23вҶ’#include <mutex>
    24вҶ’#include <nlohmann/json.hpp>
    25вҶ’#include <string>
    26вҶ’#include <unordered_map>
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/message_buffer.hpp"
    29вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/middleware/middleware_interface.hpp"
    30вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/session_manager.hpp"
    31вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/subscription_manager_interface.hpp"
    32вҶ’#include "pj_bridge/topic_source_interface.hpp"
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’/**
    37вҶ’ * @brief Main bridge server that coordinates all components
    38вҶ’ *
    39вҶ’ * Orchestrates the bridge by managing:
    40вҶ’ * - Client sessions and heartbeats
    41вҶ’ * - Topic discovery and subscriptions (via abstract interfaces)
    42вҶ’ * - Message buffering and aggregation
    43вҶ’ * - API request/response handling
    44вҶ’ *
    45вҶ’ * Thread-safe for concurrent client connections.
    46вҶ’ * Event loop is driven externally (no internal timers).
    47вҶ’ */
    48вҶ’class BridgeServer {
    49вҶ’ public:
    50вҶ’  struct StatsSnapshot {
    51вҶ’    std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_receive_counts;
    52вҶ’    std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_forward_counts;
    53вҶ’    uint64_t publish_cycles;
    54вҶ’    uint64_t total_bytes_published;
    55вҶ’  };
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /**
    58вҶ’   * @brief Constructor
    59вҶ’   * @param topic_source Backend-specific topic discovery and schema provider
    60вҶ’   * @param subscription_manager Backend-specific subscription manager
    61вҶ’   * @param middleware Middleware interface for network communication
    62вҶ’   * @param port Server port (default: 8080)
    63вҶ’   * @param session_timeout Session timeout in seconds (default: 10.0)
    64вҶ’   * @param publish_rate Message aggregation publish rate in Hz (default: 50.0)
    65вҶ’   */
    66вҶ’  explicit BridgeServer(
    67вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<TopicSourceInterface> topic_source,
    68вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<SubscriptionManagerInterface> subscription_manager,
    69вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<MiddlewareInterface> middleware, int port = 8080, double session_timeout = 10.0,
    70вҶ’      double publish_rate = 50.0);
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’  /// Shuts down middleware before members are destroyed, preventing
    73вҶ’  /// disconnect callbacks from firing into a partially destroyed object.
    74вҶ’  ~BridgeServer();
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  /**
    77вҶ’   * @brief Initialize the bridge server
    78вҶ’   * @return true if initialization successful, false otherwise
    79вҶ’   */
    80вҶ’  bool initialize();
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’  /**
    83вҶ’   * @brief Process incoming API requests
    84вҶ’   *
    85вҶ’   * Non-blocking call that checks for pending requests,
    86вҶ’   * processes them, and sends responses.
    87вҶ’   *
    88вҶ’   * @return true if a request was processed, false if no requests pending
    89вҶ’   */
    90вҶ’  bool process_requests();
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’  /**
    93вҶ’   * @brief Publish aggregated messages to connected clients
    94вҶ’   *
    95вҶ’   * Called externally by the event loop (e.g. at 50 Hz).
    96вҶ’   * Moves messages from the buffer, serializes per subscription group,
    97вҶ’   * compresses with ZSTD, and sends binary frames.
    98вҶ’   */
    99вҶ’  void publish_aggregated_messages();
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’  /**
   102вҶ’   * @brief Check for timed-out sessions and clean them up
   103вҶ’   *
   104вҶ’   * Called externally by the event loop (e.g. every 1 second).
   105вҶ’   */
   106вҶ’  void check_session_timeouts();
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’  /**
   109вҶ’   * @brief Get the number of active client sessions
   110вҶ’   */
   111вҶ’  size_t get_active_session_count() const;
   112вҶ’
   113вҶ’  /**
   114вҶ’   * @brief Get statistics about published messages
   115вҶ’   * @return Pair of (total_messages_published, total_bytes_published)
   116вҶ’   */
   117вҶ’  std::pair<uint64_t, uint64_t> get_publish_stats() const;
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’  /**
   120вҶ’   * @brief Snapshot and reset statistics counters
   121вҶ’   */
   122вҶ’  StatsSnapshot snapshot_and_reset_stats();
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’ private:
   125вҶ’  std::string handle_get_topics(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   126вҶ’  std::string handle_subscribe(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   127вҶ’  std::string handle_unsubscribe(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   128вҶ’  std::string handle_heartbeat(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   129вҶ’  std::string handle_pause(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   130вҶ’  std::string handle_resume(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   131вҶ’  std::string create_error_response(
   132вҶ’      const std::string& error_code, const std::string& message, const nlohmann::json& request) const;
   133вҶ’  void inject_response_fields(nlohmann::json& response, const nlohmann::json& request) const;
   134вҶ’  void cleanup_session(const std::string& client_id);
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’  // Backend interfaces (owned)
   137вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<TopicSourceInterface> topic_source_;
   138вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<SubscriptionManagerInterface> subscription_manager_;
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’  // Core components
   141вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<MiddlewareInterface> middleware_;
   142вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<MessageBuffer> message_buffer_;
   143вҶ’  std::unique_ptr<SessionManager> session_manager_;
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’  // Configuration
   146вҶ’  int port_;
   147вҶ’  double session_timeout_;
   148вҶ’  double publish_rate_;
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’  // State
   151вҶ’  bool initialized_;
   152вҶ’
   153вҶ’  // Statistics
   154вҶ’  uint64_t total_messages_published_;
   155вҶ’  uint64_t total_bytes_published_;
   156вҶ’  uint64_t publish_cycles_;
   157вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_receive_counts_;
   158вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_forward_counts_;
   159вҶ’  mutable std::mutex stats_mutex_;
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’  // Lock ordering (to prevent deadlock):
   162вҶ’  //   cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_
   163вҶ’  // SessionManager::mutex_ may be acquired while holding any of these.
   164вҶ’  // Never acquire a higher-order lock while holding a lower-order one.
   165вҶ’  std::mutex cleanup_mutex_;
   166вҶ’
   167вҶ’  // Per-client per-topic last-sent timestamp (nanoseconds) for rate limiting
   168вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t>> last_sent_times_;
   169вҶ’  std::mutex last_sent_mutex_;
   170вҶ’};
   171вҶ’

    18вҶ’ */
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <cstdint>
    23вҶ’#include <functional>
    24вҶ’#include <string>
    25вҶ’#include <vector>
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’#include "tl/expected.hpp"
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/// Abstract transport layer between BridgeServer and clients.
    32вҶ’///
    33вҶ’/// Implementations handle connection management and bidirectional messaging.
    34вҶ’/// The interface separates two data channels:
    35вҶ’///   - **Text** (request/reply): JSON API commands (subscribe, heartbeat, etc.)
    36вҶ’///   - **Binary** (per-client push): ZSTD-compressed aggregated message frames
    37вҶ’///
    38вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from multiple threads.
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls receive_request() and send_reply() from the request-processing
    40вҶ’/// loop, and send_binary() from the publish loop, potentially concurrently.
    41вҶ’class MiddlewareInterface {
    42вҶ’ public:
    43вҶ’  virtual ~MiddlewareInterface() = default;
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’  /// Start listening on the given port. Must be called before any other method.
    46вҶ’  /// @return void on success, or an error string describing the failure.
    47вҶ’  virtual tl::expected<void, std::string> initialize(uint16_t port) = 0;
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’  /// Stop the server and close all client connections.
    50вҶ’  /// After this call, is_ready() must return false.
    51вҶ’  virtual void shutdown() = 0;
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’  /// Poll for the next incoming text request (non-blocking or short-blocking).
    54вҶ’  /// @param[out] data       the raw request bytes (UTF-8 JSON)
    55вҶ’  /// @param[out] client_identity  opaque client identifier for send_reply()
    56вҶ’  /// @return true if a request was dequeued, false if the queue was empty.
    57вҶ’  virtual bool receive_request(std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::string& client_identity) = 0;
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’  /// Send a text reply to a specific client.
    60вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    61вҶ’  virtual bool send_reply(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’  /// Broadcast binary data to all connected clients.
    64вҶ’  /// @return true if at least one client received the data.
    65вҶ’  virtual bool publish_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’  /// Send binary data to a specific client (used for per-client aggregated frames).
    68вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    69вҶ’  virtual bool send_binary(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if initialize() succeeded and shutdown() has not been called.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool is_ready() const = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  using ConnectionCallback = std::function<void(const std::string& client_id)>;
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a new client connects.
    77вҶ’  virtual void set_on_connect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a client disconnects.
    80вҶ’  /// BridgeServer uses this to trigger session cleanup.
    81вҶ’  virtual void set_on_disconnect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    82вҶ’};
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
    85вҶ’

    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’/// A discovered topic with its fully-qualified name and message type.
    28вҶ’struct TopicInfo {
    29вҶ’  std::string name;  ///< e.g. "/sensor/imu"
    30вҶ’  std::string type;  ///< e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu"
    31вҶ’};
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/// Abstract interface for discovering topics and retrieving their schemas.
    34вҶ’///
    35вҶ’/// Implementations wrap backend-specific discovery mechanisms:
    36вҶ’///   - ROS2: `Ros2TopicSource` uses rclcpp topic enumeration + .msg file parsing
    37вҶ’///   - RTI:  `RtiTopicSource` uses DDS participant discovery + OMG IDL type info
    38вҶ’///
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls get_topics() to list available topics for clients, and
    40вҶ’/// get_schema() before subscribing to provide the client with the schema needed
    41вҶ’/// to deserialize incoming messages.
    42вҶ’class TopicSourceInterface {
    43вҶ’ public:
    44вҶ’  virtual ~TopicSourceInterface() = default;
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’  /// Return all currently available topics.
    47вҶ’  /// May be called repeatedly (e.g. on each client "get_topics" request).
    48вҶ’  virtual std::vector<TopicInfo> get_topics() = 0;
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  /// Return the full schema definition for a topic.
    51вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name
    52вҶ’  /// @return the schema text (e.g. concatenated .msg definitions for ROS2,
    53вҶ’  ///         OMG IDL for RTI). Empty string if the schema cannot be resolved.
    54вҶ’  /// @throws std::exception on unrecoverable schema extraction errors.
    55вҶ’  virtual std::string get_schema(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;

    25вҶ’#include <memory>
    26вҶ’#include <string>
    27вҶ’#include <vector>
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/// Callback invoked when a new message arrives on a subscribed topic.
    32вҶ’/// @param topic_name  the topic the message was received on
    33вҶ’/// @param cdr_data    serialized message payload (CDR-encoded)
    34вҶ’/// @param timestamp_ns  nanoseconds since epoch (source-clock timestamp)
    35вҶ’using MessageCallback = std::function<void(
    36вҶ’    const std::string& topic_name, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, uint64_t timestamp_ns)>;
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’/// Abstract interface for managing middleware subscriptions with reference counting.
    39вҶ’///
    40вҶ’/// Implementations wrap a backend-specific subscription mechanism (e.g. rclcpp
    41вҶ’/// GenericSubscription for ROS2, DDS DataReader for RTI) behind a uniform API.
    42вҶ’///
    43вҶ’/// Subscriptions are reference-counted: multiple clients can subscribe to the
    44вҶ’/// same topic, and the underlying middleware subscription is only destroyed when
    45вҶ’/// the last client unsubscribes. Each subscribe() call increments the ref count;
    46вҶ’/// each unsubscribe() call decrements it.
    47вҶ’///
    48вҶ’/// A single global MessageCallback is set once at startup. All incoming messages
    49вҶ’/// from all topics are delivered through this callback, which feeds them into
    50вҶ’/// the MessageBuffer for later aggregation and delivery.
    51вҶ’///
    52вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from any thread.
    53вҶ’class SubscriptionManagerInterface {
    54вҶ’ public:
    55вҶ’  virtual ~SubscriptionManagerInterface() = default;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Set the global callback for all incoming messages. Must be called once
    58вҶ’  /// before any subscribe() call.
    59вҶ’  virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’  /// Subscribe to a topic, incrementing its reference count.
    62вҶ’  /// If this is the first subscriber, the underlying middleware subscription is created.
    63вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name (e.g. "/sensor/imu")
    64вҶ’  /// @param topic_type  message type string (e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu");
    65вҶ’  ///                    required by ROS2, ignored by DDS backends.
    66вҶ’  /// @return true on success, false if the subscription could not be created.
    67вҶ’  virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from a topic, decrementing its reference count.
    70вҶ’  /// The underlying middleware subscription is destroyed when the count reaches zero.
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if the topic was found and ref count decremented, false otherwise.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from all topics, destroying all underlying subscriptions.
    75вҶ’  /// Called during shutdown.
    76вҶ’  virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
    77вҶ’};
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
    80вҶ’

input.prompt:
You are reviewing the pj_bridge project for correctness and potential bugs. Focus on:

Entry points:
- `ros2/src/main.cpp`
- `rti/src/main.cpp`

Test files (check for test gaps, not test style):
- `tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp`
- `tests/unit/test_message_buffer.cpp`
- `tests/unit/test_message_serializer.cpp`
- `tests/unit/test_session_manager.cpp`
- `tests/unit/test_websocket_middleware.cpp`

Read all files. Analyze for:

1. **Shutdown ordering**: Both main.cpp files have explicit shutdown sequences. Are they correct? Can use-after-free happen during shutdown? Are signal handlers safe?

2. **Lifetime issues**: Do captured references in lambdas (timers, callbacks) outlive the objects they reference? ROS2 wall timers capture `&server` вҖ” is server lifetime guaranteed?

3. **Missing test coverage for critical paths**: Are there important code paths that have no test coverage? Focus on: error paths, edge cases in subscribe/unsubscribe, concurrent operations, pause/resume interactions.

4. **Test correctness**: Are any tests testing the wrong thing or making incorrect assertions?

5. **Configuration edge cases**: What happens with port=0, publish_rate=0, session_timeout=0, negative values?

Do NOT report style issues. Only report actual bugs or correctness concerns. Cite exact file and line numbers.

This is a research-only task вҖ” do NOT modify any files.

    34вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’/**
    37вҶ’ * @brief Main bridge server that coordinates all components
    38вҶ’ *
    39вҶ’ * Orchestrates the bridge by managing:
    40вҶ’ * - Client sessions and heartbeats
    41вҶ’ * - Topic discovery and subscriptions (via abstract interfaces)
    42вҶ’ * - Message buffering and aggregation
    43вҶ’ * - API request/response handling
    44вҶ’ *
    45вҶ’ * Thread-safe for concurrent client connections.
    46вҶ’ * Event loop is driven externally (no internal timers).
    47вҶ’ */
    48вҶ’class BridgeServer {
    49вҶ’ public:
    50вҶ’  struct StatsSnapshot {
    51вҶ’    std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_receive_counts;
    52вҶ’    std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_forward_counts;
    53вҶ’    uint64_t publish_cycles;
    54вҶ’    uint64_t total_bytes_published;
    55вҶ’  };
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /**
    58вҶ’   * @brief Constructor
    59вҶ’   * @param topic_source Backend-specific topic discovery and schema provider
    60вҶ’   * @param subscription_manager Backend-specific subscription manager
    61вҶ’   * @param middleware Middleware interface for network communication
    62вҶ’   * @param port Server port (default: 8080)
    63вҶ’   * @param session_timeout Session timeout in seconds (default: 10.0)
    64вҶ’   * @param publish_rate Message aggregation publish rate in Hz (default: 50.0)
    65вҶ’   */
    66вҶ’  explicit BridgeServer(
    67вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<TopicSourceInterface> topic_source,
    68вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<SubscriptionManagerInterface> subscription_manager,
    69вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<MiddlewareInterface> middleware, int port = 8080, double session_timeout = 10.0,
    70вҶ’      double publish_rate = 50.0);
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’  /// Shuts down middleware before members are destroyed, preventing
    73вҶ’  /// disconnect callbacks from firing into a partially destroyed object.
    74вҶ’  ~BridgeServer();
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  /**
    77вҶ’   * @brief Initialize the bridge server
    78вҶ’   * @return true if initialization successful, false otherwise
    79вҶ’   */
    80вҶ’  bool initialize();
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’  /**
    83вҶ’   * @brief Process incoming API requests
    84вҶ’   *
    85вҶ’   * Non-blocking call that checks for pending requests,
    86вҶ’   * processes them, and sends responses.
    87вҶ’   *
    88вҶ’   * @return true if a request was processed, false if no requests pending
    89вҶ’   */
    90вҶ’  bool process_requests();
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’  /**
    93вҶ’   * @brief Publish aggregated messages to connected clients
    94вҶ’   *
    95вҶ’   * Called externally by the event loop (e.g. at 50 Hz).
    96вҶ’   * Moves messages from the buffer, serializes per subscription group,
    97вҶ’   * compresses with ZSTD, and sends binary frames.
    98вҶ’   */
    99вҶ’  void publish_aggregated_messages();
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’  /**
   102вҶ’   * @brief Check for timed-out sessions and clean them up
   103вҶ’   *
   104вҶ’   * Called externally by the event loop (e.g. every 1 second).
   105вҶ’   */
   106вҶ’  void check_session_timeouts();
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’  /**
   109вҶ’   * @brief Get the number of active client sessions
   110вҶ’   */
   111вҶ’  size_t get_active_session_count() const;
   112вҶ’
   113вҶ’  /**
   114вҶ’   * @brief Get statistics about published messages
   115вҶ’   * @return Pair of (total_messages_published, total_bytes_published)
   116вҶ’   */
   117вҶ’  std::pair<uint64_t, uint64_t> get_publish_stats() const;
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’  /**
   120вҶ’   * @brief Snapshot and reset statistics counters
   121вҶ’   */
   122вҶ’  StatsSnapshot snapshot_and_reset_stats();
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’ private:
   125вҶ’  std::string handle_get_topics(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   126вҶ’  std::string handle_subscribe(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   127вҶ’  std::string handle_unsubscribe(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   128вҶ’  std::string handle_heartbeat(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   129вҶ’  std::string handle_pause(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   130вҶ’  std::string handle_resume(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   131вҶ’  std::string create_error_response(
   132вҶ’      const std::string& error_code, const std::string& message, const nlohmann::json& request) const;
   133вҶ’  void inject_response_fields(nlohmann::json& response, const nlohmann::json& request) const;
   134вҶ’  void cleanup_session(const std::string& client_id);
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’  // Backend interfaces (owned)
   137вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<TopicSourceInterface> topic_source_;
   138вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<SubscriptionManagerInterface> subscription_manager_;
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’  // Core components
   141вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<MiddlewareInterface> middleware_;
   142вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<MessageBuffer> message_buffer_;
   143вҶ’  std::unique_ptr<SessionManager> session_manager_;
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’  // Configuration
   146вҶ’  int port_;
   147вҶ’  double session_timeout_;
   148вҶ’  double publish_rate_;
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’  // State
   151вҶ’  bool initialized_;
   152вҶ’
   153вҶ’  // Statistics
   154вҶ’  uint64_t total_messages_published_;
   155вҶ’  uint64_t total_bytes_published_;
   156вҶ’  uint64_t publish_cycles_;
   157вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_receive_counts_;
   158вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_forward_counts_;
   159вҶ’  mutable std::mutex stats_mutex_;
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’  // Lock ordering (to prevent deadlock):
   162вҶ’  //   cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_
   163вҶ’  // SessionManager::mutex_ may be acquired while holding any of these.
   164вҶ’  // Never acquire a higher-order lock while holding a lower-order one.
   165вҶ’  std::mutex cleanup_mutex_;
   166вҶ’
   167вҶ’  // Per-client per-topic last-sent timestamp (nanoseconds) for rate limiting
   168вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t>> last_sent_times_;
   169вҶ’  std::mutex last_sent_mutex_;
   170вҶ’};

    22вҶ’#include <memory>

    34вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’/**
    37вҶ’ * @brief Main bridge server that coordinates all components
    38вҶ’ *
    39вҶ’ * Orchestrates the bridge by managing:
    40вҶ’ * - Client sessions and heartbeats
    41вҶ’ * - Topic discovery and subscriptions (via abstract interfaces)
    42вҶ’ * - Message buffering and aggregation
    43вҶ’ * - API request/response handling
    44вҶ’ *
    45вҶ’ * Thread-safe for concurrent client connections.
    46вҶ’ * Event loop is driven externally (no internal timers).
    47вҶ’ */
    48вҶ’class BridgeServer {
    49вҶ’ public:
    50вҶ’  struct StatsSnapshot {
    51вҶ’    std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_receive_counts;
    52вҶ’    std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_forward_counts;
    53вҶ’    uint64_t publish_cycles;
    54вҶ’    uint64_t total_bytes_published;
    55вҶ’  };
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /**
    58вҶ’   * @brief Constructor
    59вҶ’   * @param topic_source Backend-specific topic discovery and schema provider
    60вҶ’   * @param subscription_manager Backend-specific subscription manager
    61вҶ’   * @param middleware Middleware interface for network communication
    62вҶ’   * @param port Server port (default: 8080)
    63вҶ’   * @param session_timeout Session timeout in seconds (default: 10.0)
    64вҶ’   * @param publish_rate Message aggregation publish rate in Hz (default: 50.0)
    65вҶ’   */
    66вҶ’  explicit BridgeServer(
    67вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<TopicSourceInterface> topic_source,
    68вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<SubscriptionManagerInterface> subscription_manager,
    69вҶ’      std::shared_ptr<MiddlewareInterface> middleware, int port = 8080, double session_timeout = 10.0,
    70вҶ’      double publish_rate = 50.0);
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’  /// Shuts down middleware before members are destroyed, preventing
    73вҶ’  /// disconnect callbacks from firing into a partially destroyed object.
    74вҶ’  ~BridgeServer();
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  /**
    77вҶ’   * @brief Initialize the bridge server
    78вҶ’   * @return true if initialization successful, false otherwise
    79вҶ’   */
    80вҶ’  bool initialize();
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’  /**
    83вҶ’   * @brief Process incoming API requests
    84вҶ’   *
    85вҶ’   * Non-blocking call that checks for pending requests,
    86вҶ’   * processes them, and sends responses.
    87вҶ’   *
    88вҶ’   * @return true if a request was processed, false if no requests pending
    89вҶ’   */
    90вҶ’  bool process_requests();
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’  /**
    93вҶ’   * @brief Publish aggregated messages to connected clients
    94вҶ’   *
    95вҶ’   * Called externally by the event loop (e.g. at 50 Hz).
    96вҶ’   * Moves messages from the buffer, serializes per subscription group,
    97вҶ’   * compresses with ZSTD, and sends binary frames.
    98вҶ’   */
    99вҶ’  void publish_aggregated_messages();
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’  /**
   102вҶ’   * @brief Check for timed-out sessions and clean them up
   103вҶ’   *
   104вҶ’   * Called externally by the event loop (e.g. every 1 second).
   105вҶ’   */
   106вҶ’  void check_session_timeouts();
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’  /**
   109вҶ’   * @brief Get the number of active client sessions
   110вҶ’   */
   111вҶ’  size_t get_active_session_count() const;
   112вҶ’
   113вҶ’  /**
   114вҶ’   * @brief Get statistics about published messages
   115вҶ’   * @return Pair of (total_messages_published, total_bytes_published)
   116вҶ’   */
   117вҶ’  std::pair<uint64_t, uint64_t> get_publish_stats() const;
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’  /**
   120вҶ’   * @brief Snapshot and reset statistics counters
   121вҶ’   */
   122вҶ’  StatsSnapshot snapshot_and_reset_stats();
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’ private:
   125вҶ’  std::string handle_get_topics(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   126вҶ’  std::string handle_subscribe(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   127вҶ’  std::string handle_unsubscribe(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   128вҶ’  std::string handle_heartbeat(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   129вҶ’  std::string handle_pause(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);
   130вҶ’  std::string handle_resume(const std::string& client_id, const nlohmann::json& request);

   132вҶ’      const std::string& error_code, const std::string& message, const nlohmann::json& request) const;
   133вҶ’  void inject_response_fields(nlohmann::json& response, const nlohmann::json& request) const;
   134вҶ’  void cleanup_session(const std::string& client_id);
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’  // Backend interfaces (owned)
   137вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<TopicSourceInterface> topic_source_;
   138вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<SubscriptionManagerInterface> subscription_manager_;
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’  // Core components
   141вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<MiddlewareInterface> middleware_;
   142вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<MessageBuffer> message_buffer_;
   143вҶ’  std::unique_ptr<SessionManager> session_manager_;
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’  // Configuration
   146вҶ’  int port_;
   147вҶ’  double session_timeout_;
   148вҶ’  double publish_rate_;
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’  // State
   151вҶ’  bool initialized_;
   152вҶ’
   153вҶ’  // Statistics
   154вҶ’  uint64_t total_messages_published_;
   155вҶ’  uint64_t total_bytes_published_;
   156вҶ’  uint64_t publish_cycles_;
   157вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_receive_counts_;
   158вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t> topic_forward_counts_;
   159вҶ’  mutable std::mutex stats_mutex_;
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’  // Lock ordering (to prevent deadlock):
   162вҶ’  //   cleanup_mutex_ > last_sent_mutex_ > stats_mutex_
   163вҶ’  // SessionManager::mutex_ may be acquired while holding any of these.
   164вҶ’  // Never acquire a higher-order lock while holding a lower-order one.
   165вҶ’  std::mutex cleanup_mutex_;
   166вҶ’
   167вҶ’  // Per-client per-topic last-sent timestamp (nanoseconds) for rate limiting
   168вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, std::unordered_map<std::string, uint64_t>> last_sent_times_;
   169вҶ’  std::mutex last_sent_mutex_;
   170вҶ’};

   172вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    31вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/// A single message held in the buffer, waiting to be aggregated and sent.
    34вҶ’struct BufferedMessage {
    35вҶ’  uint64_t timestamp_ns;    ///< Source-clock timestamp from the middleware
    36вҶ’  uint64_t received_at_ns;  ///< Wall-clock time when add_message() was called (used for TTL cleanup)
    37вҶ’  std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> data;  ///< CDR-encoded message payload
    38вҶ’};
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’/// Thread-safe per-topic message buffer with time-based auto-cleanup.
    41вҶ’///
    42вҶ’/// Messages arrive via add_message() (called from the SubscriptionManager callback)
    43вҶ’/// and are drained by move_messages() (called from BridgeServer::publish_aggregated_messages()).
    44вҶ’///
    45вҶ’/// Stale messages older than max_message_age_ns are automatically removed on each
    46вҶ’/// add_message() call. Cleanup uses received_at_ns (wall-clock), not timestamp_ns,
    47вҶ’/// so sim-time offsets don't cause premature eviction or unbounded growth.
    48вҶ’///
    49вҶ’/// Thread safety: all public methods are mutex-protected. add_message() may be
    50вҶ’/// called concurrently with move_messages() from different threads.
    51вҶ’class MessageBuffer {
    52вҶ’ public:
    53вҶ’  static constexpr uint64_t kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs = 1'000'000'000;  ///< 1 second
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’  /// @param max_message_age_ns  TTL for buffered messages (default 1 second).
    56вҶ’  explicit MessageBuffer(uint64_t max_message_age_ns = kDefaultMaxMessageAgeNs);
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’  /// Add a message to the buffer for the given topic.
    59вҶ’  /// Triggers cleanup of stale messages before inserting.
    60вҶ’  /// @param topic_name    fully-qualified topic name
    61вҶ’  /// @param timestamp_ns  source-clock timestamp from the middleware
    62вҶ’  /// @param data          CDR-encoded message payload (shared ownership)
    63вҶ’  void add_message(const std::string& topic_name, uint64_t timestamp_ns, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> data);
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’  /// Atomically drain all buffered messages into @p out_messages.
    66вҶ’  /// After this call the internal buffer is empty. Messages are appended to any
    67вҶ’  /// existing entries in out_messages (keyed by topic name).
    68вҶ’  void move_messages(std::unordered_map<std::string, std::deque<BufferedMessage>>& out_messages);
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’  /// Discard all buffered messages across all topics.
    71вҶ’  void clear();
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’  /// Return the total number of messages across all topics.
    74вҶ’  size_t size() const;
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’ private:
    77вҶ’  mutable std::mutex mutex_;
    78вҶ’  std::unordered_map<std::string, std::deque<BufferedMessage>> topic_buffers_;
    79вҶ’  uint64_t last_read_timestamp_ns_;
    80вҶ’  uint64_t max_message_age_ns_;
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’  void cleanup_old_messages();
    83вҶ’};

    20вҶ’#pragma once
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’#include <cstddef>
    23вҶ’#include <cstdint>

    25вҶ’#include <memory>

    29вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/// Callback invoked when a new message arrives on a subscribed topic.
    32вҶ’/// @param topic_name  the topic the message was received on
    33вҶ’/// @param cdr_data    serialized message payload (CDR-encoded)
    34вҶ’/// @param timestamp_ns  nanoseconds since epoch (source-clock timestamp)
    35вҶ’using MessageCallback = std::function<void(
    36вҶ’    const std::string& topic_name, std::shared_ptr<std::vector<std::byte>> cdr_data, uint64_t timestamp_ns)>;
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’/// Abstract interface for managing middleware subscriptions with reference counting.
    39вҶ’///
    40вҶ’/// Implementations wrap a backend-specific subscription mechanism (e.g. rclcpp
    41вҶ’/// GenericSubscription for ROS2, DDS DataReader for RTI) behind a uniform API.
    42вҶ’///
    43вҶ’/// Subscriptions are reference-counted: multiple clients can subscribe to the
    44вҶ’/// same topic, and the underlying middleware subscription is only destroyed when
    45вҶ’/// the last client unsubscribes. Each subscribe() call increments the ref count;
    46вҶ’/// each unsubscribe() call decrements it.
    47вҶ’///
    48вҶ’/// A single global MessageCallback is set once at startup. All incoming messages
    49вҶ’/// from all topics are delivered through this callback, which feeds them into
    50вҶ’/// the MessageBuffer for later aggregation and delivery.
    51вҶ’///
    52вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from any thread.
    53вҶ’class SubscriptionManagerInterface {
    54вҶ’ public:
    55вҶ’  virtual ~SubscriptionManagerInterface() = default;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Set the global callback for all incoming messages. Must be called once
    58вҶ’  /// before any subscribe() call.
    59вҶ’  virtual void set_message_callback(MessageCallback callback) = 0;
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’  /// Subscribe to a topic, incrementing its reference count.
    62вҶ’  /// If this is the first subscriber, the underlying middleware subscription is created.
    63вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name (e.g. "/sensor/imu")
    64вҶ’  /// @param topic_type  message type string (e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu");
    65вҶ’  ///                    required by ROS2, ignored by DDS backends.
    66вҶ’  /// @return true on success, false if the subscription could not be created.
    67вҶ’  virtual bool subscribe(const std::string& topic_name, const std::string& topic_type) = 0;
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from a topic, decrementing its reference count.
    70вҶ’  /// The underlying middleware subscription is destroyed when the count reaches zero.
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if the topic was found and ref count decremented, false otherwise.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool unsubscribe(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  /// Unsubscribe from all topics, destroying all underlying subscriptions.
    75вҶ’  /// Called during shutdown.
    76вҶ’  virtual void unsubscribe_all() = 0;
    77вҶ’};
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’/// A discovered topic with its fully-qualified name and message type.
    28вҶ’struct TopicInfo {
    29вҶ’  std::string name;  ///< e.g. "/sensor/imu"
    30вҶ’  std::string type;  ///< e.g. "sensor_msgs/msg/Imu"
    31вҶ’};
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/// Abstract interface for discovering topics and retrieving their schemas.
    34вҶ’///
    35вҶ’/// Implementations wrap backend-specific discovery mechanisms:
    36вҶ’///   - ROS2: `Ros2TopicSource` uses rclcpp topic enumeration + .msg file parsing
    37вҶ’///   - RTI:  `RtiTopicSource` uses DDS participant discovery + OMG IDL type info
    38вҶ’///
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls get_topics() to list available topics for clients, and
    40вҶ’/// get_schema() before subscribing to provide the client with the schema needed
    41вҶ’/// to deserialize incoming messages.
    42вҶ’class TopicSourceInterface {
    43вҶ’ public:
    44вҶ’  virtual ~TopicSourceInterface() = default;
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’  /// Return all currently available topics.
    47вҶ’  /// May be called repeatedly (e.g. on each client "get_topics" request).
    48вҶ’  virtual std::vector<TopicInfo> get_topics() = 0;
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’  /// Return the full schema definition for a topic.
    51вҶ’  /// @param topic_name  fully-qualified topic name
    52вҶ’  /// @return the schema text (e.g. concatenated .msg definitions for ROS2,
    53вҶ’  ///         OMG IDL for RTI). Empty string if the schema cannot be resolved.
    54вҶ’  /// @throws std::exception on unrecoverable schema extraction errors.
    55вҶ’  virtual std::string get_schema(const std::string& topic_name) = 0;
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’  /// Return the encoding identifier for schemas produced by this source.
    58вҶ’  /// Used in the subscribe response so clients know how to parse the schema.
    59вҶ’  /// @return "ros2msg" for ROS2, "omgidl" for RTI DDS.
    60вҶ’  virtual std::string schema_encoding() const = 0;
    61вҶ’};
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge

    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/// Abstract transport layer between BridgeServer and clients.
    32вҶ’///
    33вҶ’/// Implementations handle connection management and bidirectional messaging.
    34вҶ’/// The interface separates two data channels:
    35вҶ’///   - **Text** (request/reply): JSON API commands (subscribe, heartbeat, etc.)
    36вҶ’///   - **Binary** (per-client push): ZSTD-compressed aggregated message frames
    37вҶ’///
    38вҶ’/// Thread safety: implementations must be safe to call from multiple threads.
    39вҶ’/// BridgeServer calls receive_request() and send_reply() from the request-processing
    40вҶ’/// loop, and send_binary() from the publish loop, potentially concurrently.
    41вҶ’class MiddlewareInterface {
    42вҶ’ public:
    43вҶ’  virtual ~MiddlewareInterface() = default;
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’  /// Start listening on the given port. Must be called before any other method.
    46вҶ’  /// @return void on success, or an error string describing the failure.
    47вҶ’  virtual tl::expected<void, std::string> initialize(uint16_t port) = 0;
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’  /// Stop the server and close all client connections.
    50вҶ’  /// After this call, is_ready() must return false.
    51вҶ’  virtual void shutdown() = 0;
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’  /// Poll for the next incoming text request (non-blocking or short-blocking).
    54вҶ’  /// @param[out] data       the raw request bytes (UTF-8 JSON)
    55вҶ’  /// @param[out] client_identity  opaque client identifier for send_reply()
    56вҶ’  /// @return true if a request was dequeued, false if the queue was empty.
    57вҶ’  virtual bool receive_request(std::vector<uint8_t>& data, std::string& client_identity) = 0;
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’  /// Send a text reply to a specific client.
    60вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    61вҶ’  virtual bool send_reply(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’  /// Broadcast binary data to all connected clients.
    64вҶ’  /// @return true if at least one client received the data.
    65вҶ’  virtual bool publish_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’  /// Send binary data to a specific client (used for per-client aggregated frames).
    68вҶ’  /// @return true if the message was sent, false if the client is gone.
    69вҶ’  virtual bool send_binary(const std::string& client_identity, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) = 0;
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’  /// @return true if initialize() succeeded and shutdown() has not been called.
    72вҶ’  virtual bool is_ready() const = 0;
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’  using ConnectionCallback = std::function<void(const std::string& client_id)>;
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a new client connects.
    77вҶ’  virtual void set_on_connect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’  /// Register a callback invoked when a client disconnects.
    80вҶ’  /// BridgeServer uses this to trigger session cleanup.
    81вҶ’  virtual void set_on_disconnect(ConnectionCallback callback) = 0;
    82вҶ’};
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’}  // namespace pj_bridge
    85вҶ’

content.text:
Now I have a thorough understanding of both files and how they interact. Let me compile a detailed analysis.

---

## Bug / Correctness Analysis: `WebSocketMiddleware`

### Files reviewed:
- `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/include/pj_bridge/middleware/websocket_middleware.hpp`
- `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp`
- `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/include/pj_bridge/middleware/middleware_interface.hpp`
- `/home/davide/ws_plotjuggler/src/pj_ros_bridge/app/src/bridge_server.cpp`

---

### BUG 1: `receive_request()` can block indefinitely after `shutdown()` due to a missed wakeup

**File:** `websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 193-214

**Description:** There is a TOCTOU race between the `initialized_` check (line 196) and the `wait_for` call (line 203-204). Consider this interleaving:

1. Thread A (caller) enters `receive_request()`, acquires `state_mutex_`, reads `initialized_ == true`, releases `state_mutex_` (line 199).
2. Thread B calls `shutdown()`, sets `initialized_ = false` (line 129), calls `queue_cv_.notify_all()` (line 135).
3. Thread A now acquires `queue_mutex_` and enters `wait_for` (line 203). The `notify_all()` from step 2 has already fired and been missed.

The mitigation is that `wait_for` has a 10ms timeout (`kReceiveTimeoutMs`), so Thread A will wake up after 10ms, find the queue empty, and return `false`. This is not a hang, but it means `receive_request()` can return stale `true`-for-initialized results and delay shutdown responsiveness by up to 10ms. Since the timeout is short, this is a **low-severity issue** -- functionally correct in practice, just slightly inelegant. The predicate in `wait_for` only checks `!incoming_queue_.empty()`, not `!initialized_`, so a spurious message enqueued between steps 1 and 3 could still be returned after shutdown. This is benign since the caller (BridgeServer) will get a `false` from `send_reply` and clean up.

**Impact:** Low. 10ms maximum delay during shutdown. No hang or data corruption.

---

### BUG 2: Unbounded incoming queue growth under message flooding

**File:** `websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 93-101

**Description:** When a text message arrives in the `OnClientMessage` callback, it is unconditionally pushed into `incoming_queue_` (line 99). There is no size limit on this queue. If a malicious or misbehaving client sends text messages faster than the event loop consumes them via `receive_request()`, the queue will grow without bound, consuming increasing memory.

The event loop calls `receive_request()` which pops one message at a time, and `process_requests()` in `BridgeServer` calls `receive_request()` once per invocation. If the event loop runs at, say, 50 Hz with one `process_requests()` call per cycle, and a client sends thousands of messages per second, the queue will grow.

**Impact:** Medium. Memory exhaustion under adversarial or pathological client behavior. A production system exposed to untrusted clients could be DoSed.

---

### BUG 3: Sending to an `ix::WebSocket` while it is being concurrently closed (data race on IXWebSocket internals)

**File:** `websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 216-240 (`send_reply`, `send_binary`)

**Description:** `send_reply()` and `send_binary()` look up the client `shared_ptr<ix::WebSocket>` under `clients_mutex_`, then call `it->second->send()` or `it->second->sendBinary()` while still holding `clients_mutex_`. Meanwhile, the `Close` callback handler (line 76-89) also acquires `clients_mutex_` to erase the client.

The current locking means `send` and `erase` cannot happen truly concurrently (they contend on `clients_mutex_`), which is correct. However, `send()` and `sendBinary()` are called while holding `clients_mutex_`, which means:

- If `ix::WebSocket::send()` blocks or takes a long time (e.g., TCP backpressure, slow client), **all** other operations that need `clients_mutex_` are blocked: new connections, disconnections, other sends, and `publish_data()`. This is a throughput bottleneck, not a correctness bug per se.

- The `publish_data()` method (lines 242-262) iterates over all clients and calls `sendBinary()` on each, all under `clients_mutex_`. One slow client blocks sends to all other clients and blocks new connection/disconnection handling.

**Impact:** Medium. Under load with a slow client, this degrades latency and responsiveness for all other clients. Not a crash, but a significant performance hazard in production.

---

### BUG 4: `getClients()` iteration during `Open` callback may miss the connecting client

**File:** `websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 57-64

**Description:** When a new client connects, the `Open` callback iterates over `server_->getClients()` to find the matching `shared_ptr<ix::WebSocket>` by comparing raw pointers (line 60: `client.get() == &web_socket`). This relies on `getClients()` returning a set that includes the newly connecting client. According to IXWebSocket internals, `getClients()` returns a snapshot of `_clients`, which should include the websocket by the time the `Open` message fires. However:

- If IXWebSocket's internal `_clients` set is modified concurrently (another client connecting/disconnecting), the snapshot obtained by `getClients()` is safe (IXWebSocket returns a copy under lock).
- The linear scan (`for` loop) is O(N) in the number of connected clients. With many clients, this could be slow. Not a correctness bug, but a scalability concern.

**Impact:** Low. The pattern is correct given IXWebSocket's documented behavior.

---

### BUG 5: Race between shutdown timeout/detach and `WebSocketMiddleware` destructor

**File:** `websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 119-191

**Description:** When `shutdown()` times out (line 169-178), the stop thread is detached. The comment correctly notes that the callback was already replaced with a no-op (line 141-142) and `server_to_stop` is captured by `shared_ptr`, so the server lives until `stop()` completes.

However, consider this sequence:
1. First `shutdown()` call times out. The stop thread is detached, still running `server_to_stop->stop()`.
2. `initialized_` is `false`, `server_` is null.
3. `initialize()` is called again (re-initialization). A new server is created on the same port.
4. The old detached stop thread is still running, and the old server is still bound to the port.

At step 3, `server_->listen()` would fail because the old server still holds the port. The `listen()` failure is handled (line 107-111), returning an error. So this does not crash, but the user gets a confusing "Failed to listen on port" error until the detached thread finishes. This could persist for an arbitrarily long time if `stop()` is truly hanging.

Also: if the `WebSocketMiddleware` object is destroyed while the detached thread is running, there is **no use-after-free** because:
- The callback was replaced with a no-op (captures nothing from `this`).
- The server is captured by `shared_ptr` in the thread.
- The `done` atomic is captured by `shared_ptr`.

This is correctly handled.

**Impact:** Low. Re-initialization after a timeout will fail until the detached thread finishes. Not a crash.

---

### BUG 6: `publish_data()` returns `false` when there are zero clients, even when not initialized

**File:** `websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 242-262

**Description:** `publish_data()` does not check `initialized_`. After `shutdown()`, `clients_` is cleared (line 182-184), so `publish_data()` returns `false` due to `clients_.empty()` on line 245. This is functionally correct but inconsistent with `receive_request()` which explicitly checks `initialized_`. The missing check means that if someone calls `publish_data()` between `shutdown()` clearing `clients_` (line 182-184) and the server actually stopping, it would still return `false` because `clients_` is already empty. No actual bug here.

Similarly, `send_reply()` and `send_binary()` don't check `initialized_`. After shutdown, `clients_` is empty, so they return `false`. Correct behavior.

**Impact:** None. Functionally equivalent.

---

### BUG 7: No protection against concurrent `initialize()` and `shutdown()`

**File:** `websocket_middleware.cpp`, lines 35-117 and 119-191

**Description:** `initialize()` acquires `state_mutex_`, creates the server, calls `server_->listen()` and `server_->start()`, then sets `initialized_ = true` -- all under `state_mutex_`. `shutdown()` acquires `state_mutex_`, sets `initialized_ = false`, and moves `server_` out. These are mutually exclusive due to `state_mutex_`, which is correct.

However, `initialize()` calls `server_->listen()` (line 106) and `server_->start()` (line 113) while holding `state_mutex_`. If `listen()` or `start()` block for a significant time or invoke callbacks synchronously, this could delay `shutdown()` or other state-checking operations. The `setOnClientMessageCallback` lambda (line 44-104) itself tries to acquire `state_mutex_` (line 48). If a client connects between `server_->start()` (line 113) and `initialized_ = true` (line 114), the callback fires, acquires `state_mutex_`, sees `initialized_ == false`, and returns early. This means the first connection attempt after `start()` but before `initialized_ = true` is silently dropped.

Actually, re-reading: `server_->start()` is called at line 113, then `initialized_ = true` at line 114, but **both are inside the `state_mutex_` lock** (acquired at line 36). So the callback at line 48 would block on `state_mutex_` until line 116 (end of the lock scope in `initialize`). By that time `initialized_` is `true`. So no connection is dropped. This is correct.

**Impact:** None.

---

### BUG 8 (Actual concern): Lock ordering between `state_mutex_` and `clients_mutex_`

**File:** `websocket_middleware.cpp`

**Description:** The `OnClientMessage` callback acquires locks in this order:
1. `state_mutex_` (line 48) -- released at line 52
2. `clients_mutex_` (line 58, for Open) or (line 87, for Close)

Meanwhile, `shutdown()` acquires:
1. `state_mutex_` (line 123) -- released at line 133
2. `clients_mutex_` (line 182) -- after server stop

Since `state_mutex_` is always acquired and released before `clients_mutex_` in both paths, there is no nested locking and thus no deadlock risk. The locks are acquired sequentially, not nested.

**Impact:** None. No deadlock.

---

### BUG 9: `send_reply` copies data into `std::string` unnecessarily

**File:** `websocket_middleware.cpp`, line 224

```cpp
std::string text_data(data.begin(), data.end());
```

This is a performance concern, not a correctness bug. The `data` vector is copied into a string just to call `send()`. Similarly in `send_binary` (line 237) and `publish_data` (line 249). Since the user asked not to report style issues, and this is more of a performance concern, I mention it only for completeness.

**Impact:** None (performance only).

---

## Summary of Findings (ranked by severity)

| # | Severity | Location | Issue |
|---|----------|----------|-------|
| 3 | **Medium** | Lines 216-262 | Sending while holding `clients_mutex_` means one slow client blocks all other operations (connection handling, other sends). Under production load with a slow client, all clients experience increased latency. |
| 2 | **Medium** | Lines 93-101 | Unbounded `incoming_queue_` growth. A misbehaving client can cause memory exhaustion by flooding text messages. |
| 1 | **Low** | Lines 193-214 | TOCTOU between `initialized_` check and `wait_for`. Mitigated by 10ms timeout. Shutdown could be delayed by up to 10ms. |
| 5 | **Low** | Lines 169-178 | Re-initialization after shutdown timeout will fail because the detached thread still holds the port. Not a crash, but confusing behavior. |

No use-after-free, no deadlocks, and no data corruption bugs were found. The shutdown logic is well-designed: the callback is replaced with a no-op before stopping, the server is captured by `shared_ptr`, and callbacks are cleared under `state_mutex_`. The biggest practical concerns are the coarse-grained `clients_mutex_` locking during sends (Bug 3) and the unbounded queue (Bug 2).

## Summary of Findings (ranked by severity)

| # | Severity | Location | Issue |
|---|----------|----------|-------|
| 3 | **Medium** | Lines 216-262 | Sending while holding `clients_mutex_` means one slow client blocks all other operations (connection handling, other sends). Under production load with a slow client, all clients experience increased latency. |
| 2 | **Medium** | Lines 93-101 | Unbounded `incoming_queue_` growth. A misbehaving client can cause memory exhaustion by flooding text messages. |
| 1 | **Low** | Lines 193-214 | TOCTOU between `initialized_` check and `wait_for`. Mitigated by 10ms timeout. Shutdown could be delayed by up to 10ms. |
| 5 | **Low** | Lines 169-178 | Re-initialization after shutdown timeout will fail because the detached thread still holds the port. Not a crash, but confusing behavior. |

No use-after-free, no deadlocks, and no data corruption bugs were found. The shutdown logic is well-designed: the callback is replaced with a no-op before stopping, the server is captured by `shared_ptr`, and callbacks are cleared under `state_mutex_`. The biggest practical concerns are the coarse-grained `clients_mutex_` locking during sends (Bug 3) and the unbounded queue (Bug 2).

    25вҶ’#include <vector>
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’namespace pj_bridge {
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’/**
    30вҶ’ * @brief Serializes aggregated messages using streaming zero-copy approach
    31вҶ’ *
    32вҶ’ * Design principles:
    33вҶ’ * - Streaming API: Messages serialized immediately to output buffer (no intermediate storage)
    34вҶ’ * - No header: Messages serialized sequentially without placeholder or count header
    35вҶ’ * - Little-endian: All multi-byte values use little-endian byte order
    36вҶ’ *
    37вҶ’ * Binary format specification (per message):
    38вҶ’ *   - Topic name length (uint16_t little-endian)
    39вҶ’ *   - Topic name (N bytes UTF-8)
    40вҶ’ *   - Timestamp (uint64_t nanoseconds since epoch, little-endian)
    41вҶ’ *   - Message data length (uint32_t little-endian)
    42вҶ’ *   - Message data (N bytes - CDR serialized)
    43вҶ’ *
    44вҶ’ * Thread safety: Not thread-safe, use from single thread only
    45вҶ’ */
    46вҶ’class AggregatedMessageSerializer {
    47вҶ’ public:

    61вҶ’   * @brief Get reference to the serialized data buffer
    62вҶ’   */

    64вҶ’    return serialized_data_;
    65вҶ’  }
